turns.content: Set up a new TypeScript project the way I like it
🗣️ Kaya: Need project name, purpose, and location before scaffolding your TypeScript project with Bun.

tool_calls.name: Read
tool_calls.result:
1→<!--
     2→================================================================================
     3→PAI CORE - USER/TECHSTACKPREFERENCES.md
     4→================================================================================
     5→
     6→PURPOSE:
     7→Your technology stack preferences. Your AI uses this to make consistent
     8→technology choices across all development work - languages, package managers,
     9→formats, and workflow patterns.
    10→
    11→LOCATION:
    12→- Kai (Private): ${PAI_DIR}/skills/CORE/USER/TECHSTACKPREFERENCES.md
    13→- PAI Pack: Packs/pai-core-install/src/skills/CORE/USER/TECHSTACKPREFERENCES.md
    14→
    15→CUSTOMIZATION:
    16→- [ ] Adjust language preferences to match your stack
    17→- [ ] Update package manager preferences
    18→- [ ] Modify format standards as needed
    19→- [ ] Add your own workflow patterns
    20→
    21→RELATED FILES:
    22→- SYSTEM/TOOLS.md - CLI utilities reference
    23→- SYSTEM/CLIFIRSTARCHITECTURE.md - CLI-first design patterns
    24→- ALGOPREFS.md - AI behavior customizations
    25→
    26→LAST UPDATED: 2026-01-08
    27→VERSION: 1.1.0
    28→================================================================================
    29→-->
    30→
    31→# Stack Preferences
    32→
    33→**Your Technology Stack Preferences - Definitive Reference**
    34→
    35→This document captures your core technology stack preferences for PAI infrastructure and all development work.
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Languages
    40→
    41→### Primary Language: Python
    42→
    43→**Primary Rule:** Use Python for data science, analysis, and general scripting.
    44→
    45→**When to Use Python:**
    46→- All new infrastructure development
    47→- Data analysis and machine learning
    48→- Command-line tools and utilities
    49→- Default choice for all new projects
    50→
    51→**When TypeScript/Other is Acceptable:**
    52→- Web development / Frontend
    53→- Config files requiring JSON/YAML/JS
    54→
    55→---
    56→
    57→## Package Managers
    58→
    59→### Python: pip
    60→
    61→**Commands:**
    62→```bash
    63→# Install packages
    64→pip install package-name
    65→
    66→# Requirements
    67→pip freeze > requirements.txt
    68→pip install -r requirements.txt
    69→```
    70→
    71→### JavaScript/TypeScript: npm/bun
    72→
    73→**Commands:**
    74→```bash
    75→# Install dependencies
    76→npm install
    77→
    78→# Add a new package
    79→npm install package-name
    80→```
    81→
    82→---
    83→
    84→## Formats & Standards
    85→
    86→### Documentation Format: Markdown
    87→
    88→**Primary Rule:** Use Markdown for all documentation.
    89→
    90→**Acceptable HTML:**
    91→- Custom components (`<aside>`, `<details>`, `<summary>`)
    92→- Interactive elements requiring specific behavior
    93→
    94→**Avoid HTML for:**
    95→- Basic paragraphs, headers, lists
    96→- Links and emphasis
    97→- Code blocks and tables
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→## Workflow Patterns
   102→
   103→### Git Practices
   104→
   105→**Style:** Commit often with simple descriptions and merge when branch work is done.
   106→
   107→**Commit Messages:**
   108→- Simple and descriptive
   109→- Example: "update user contact info" or "fix data processing bug"
   110→- Merge strategy: Merge branch when work is complete
   111→
   112→### Analysis vs Action: Explicit Intent Required
   113→
   114→**Analysis Tasks (Read-Only):**
   115→- "Analyze the authentication flow"
   116→- "Review this code for issues"
   117→- "What's wrong with this implementation?"
   118→
   119→**Action Tasks (Modifications Allowed):**
   120→- "Fix the authentication bug"
   121→- "Refactor this code"
   122→- "Implement the new feature"
   123→
   124→**Rule:** If asked to analyze, do analysis ONLY - don't change things unless explicitly asked.
   125→
   126→---
   127→
   128→## Terminal & Browser
   129→
   130→### Terminal: Terminal.app
   131→
   132→Use the standard macOS Terminal.
   133→
   134→### Browser: Google Chrome
   135→
   136→```bash
   137→# Open a URL
   138→open -a "Google Chrome" "http://localhost:5200"
   139→```
   140→
   141→---
   142→
   143→## Summary Reference Card
   144→
   145→```
   146→LANGUAGES:
   147→  Primary: Python
   148→  Secondary: TypeScript
   149→
   150→PACKAGE MANAGERS:
   151→  JS/TS: Bun
   152→  Python: Pip
   153→
   154→FORMATS:
   155→  Documentation: Markdown
   156→  Config: JSON/YAML
   157→
   158→WORKFLOW:
   159→  Analysis → Read only, report findings
   160→  Action → Modify with confidence
   161→```
   162→
   163→---
   164→
   165→**This is the definitive reference for stack preferences. When in doubt, consult this document.**
   166→
